title: sarcocephalus subditus
intro: Sarcocephalus subditus, also known as the African teak or African mahogany, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The bark and roots of the Sarcocephalus subditus plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as to aid in digestion and alleviate respiratory problems. Sarcocephalus subditus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves and roots are also used in traditional medicine to create various remedies.
